entries.title: COMMENTARY ON REVELATION on revelation 9:12
entries.excerpt: One woe is past. That is, it preceded in the narration, or was actually fulfilled in the past. Behold there come yet two woes more hereafter. That is, in the narration, or they are actually to be fulfilled in the future.

entries.title: Commentary on Revelation on revelation 9:12
entries.excerpt: One woe is past, and behold, two woes are still to come. Since he predicted that three woes were to come, he now mentions that one has already come in heretical deceit, but two remain, which will occur in the time of the Antichrist and on the day of judgment for the wicked.

entries.title: Commentary on Revelation on revelation 9:12
entries.excerpt: "Woe", he said, "one has gone; behold, two more woes are coming". The woe [οὐαὶ] as an forceful expression indicates the severe afflictions present and those to come upon them. Therefore, he says that one punishment has been declared; two more remain to be spoken of.
